name: Beef Jerky
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim all fat from the flank steak.
2. Semi-freeze the meat and slice it into strips that are 1/8 inch thick.
3. Marinate the slices overnight in the refrigerator, turning them occasionally to ensure even coating.
4. Drain the marinated slices well on paper towels and pat dry with a cloth or paper towel.
5. Arrange the slices on an oven rack without overlapping. Place a cookie sheet on the lower oven rack to catch any drips.
6. Roast at 125 to 140 degrees Fahrenheit for 8 to 10 hours, keeping the oven door slightly open. Taste one slice periodically to check for desired chewiness.
7. Store the finished jerky in an airtight brown paper bag.
